title: Reaction Rate Kinetics — Arrhenius Equation
content: The rate constant k depends exponentially on temperature: k = A·exp(−Ea/RT), where A is the pre-exponential factor, Ea the activation energy (J/mol), R the gas constant, and T absolute temperature (K). A 10 °C rise approximately doubles most reaction rates (Q10 rule). Sets hard upper temperature limits for safe reactor operation and informs PID setpoint selection.
